summary: Independent QA review accepts the M3 task graph. The DAG covers Roadmap and acceptance-matrix M3 requirements, preserves downstream DRAFT status, orders historical claim-pipeline and systems prerequisites before dependent implementation work, confines changed paths to planning/coordination scope, and leaves M3 implementation unstarted.

tests_run.result: Modified coordination files present: project/goal-mode-state.json, project/model-routing-state.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json. Untracked planning/routing files present under project/tasks/active/M3-*.json, project/messages/outbox, and project/messages/routes. No apps, packages, content-source, docs, or forbidden product paths appeared.
M0/M1/M2 tasks listed CLOSED; M3-TASK-GRAPH-001 listed REVIEW; all 12 downstream M3 tasks listed DRAFT.
No ready-task output while M3-TASK-GRAPH-001 remains in REVIEW.

tests_run.command: $failed = $false; Get-ChildItem project/tasks/active/M3-*.json | Sort-Object Name | ForEach-Object { node .agents/skills/monsoon-studio-orchestrator/scripts/taskctl.mjs validate $_.BaseName; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { $failed = $true } }; if ($failed) { exit 1 }
tests_run.result: All 13 M3 task JSON files validated successfully with taskctl.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON/DAG/scope validation for all M3 task files, dependency ordering, downstream status, historical prerequisite, H003/H005 dependencies, exit dependencies, and changed path scope
tests_run.result: M3 DAG/scope check ok files=13 changed_paths=18 statuses=1 REVIEW/12 DRAFT.
Tracked modifications are limited to coordination state: project/goal-mode-state.json, project/model-routing-state.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json.

decisions: ACCEPT: M3 task graph represents Roadmap M3 and acceptance-matrix coverage for polity/vassal chains, obligations, administrative burden, 50-80 characters/relationships/offices, appointments/enfeoffment/policies, tribute/troop fulfillment, postwar arrangements, minimal succession, appointment/bulk/reason UI, H-003/H-005 initial validation, historical claim pipeline, save/runtime support, and M3 exit validation.

Dependency ordering is acceptable: M3-HISTORICAL-CLAIM-PIPELINE-001 and M3-POLITY-VASSALAGE-001 are the first downstream prerequisites after graph acceptance; character/office fixture depends on both; appointments, obligations, succession, postwar governance, save/runtime, UI, H003/H005 validation, and exit validation are ordered after their required substrates.
Historical claim-pipeline safety is present before names, titles, kinship, offices, and relationship fixture work; downstream tasks forbid formal historical content unless claim-labeled and reviewed.
Allowed and forbidden paths for M3-TASK-GRAPH-001 are strict enough for planning only; observed workspace changes are confined to task JSON, message/routing, and coordination-state paths.
Downstream M3 tasks remain DRAFT and taskctl ready has no output, so implementation has not started.
risks: project/goal-mode-state.json, project/model-routing-state.json, and project/tasks/thread-registry.json are modified coordination files that pre-existed this QA review; they are within M3-TASK-GRAPH-001 allowed paths but should still be checked by lead before integration.
project/messages/routes/M3-TASK-GRAPH-001__ROUTE-20260625-LEAD-TO-QA.json is untracked and within project/messages/**; it is not a blocker.
This review validates graph completeness and safety only; it does not validate any future M3 implementation behavior.
requested_action: Integrate the accepted M3 task graph, update task/state through the lead-owned workflow, and select the next dependency-ready M3 task without starting implementation before lead routing.
